spec_match.rationale: The demo now models the extendedLifetime owner-disconnect timeout, reconnect-during-grace behavior, timeout expiry, and same-URL/name option-mismatch edge called out by the WHATWG change while probing the real SharedWorker constructor.

interactive.rationale: Users can connect and disconnect both worker models, adjust the simulated UA timeout with a range input, run edge scenarios from a select, and execute a live constructor probe.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page promises a side-by-side keepalive timer for standard versus extended-lifetime shared workers, and the revised controls keep that comparison central.

edge_cases.rationale: Unsupported SharedWorker contexts degrade to a visible fallback, reconnecting during grace cancels the timeout, timeout expiry is scriptable, and the mismatch probe surfaces the same-name extendedLifetime conflict path.

references.rationale: The references section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5138641357373440.

design_system.rationale: The remaining raw hex script colors were replaced with CSS-variable-backed classes, and local button styles continue to set both background and color.

summary: The keepalive timer now clears the major critique: it includes the required ChromeStatus reference and adds real fallback/probe paths plus configurable edge scenarios without leaving the CSS-variable design system.
